name: marp-slides
description: Creates MARP presentation decks (.md files rendered to PDF/HTML/PPTX via marp CLI) with custom CSS themes, SVG inline charts, dashboard components, and speaker notes.

name: debriefing-code-changes
description: Debriefs a developer after an AI-assisted coding session by inspecting git diffs or commits, explaining the actual architecture decisions, design patterns, tradeoffs, caveats, and learning concepts with file/function references, then generating a practical quiz and follow-up study notes.

name: fix-from-logs
description: Diagnoses bugs from raw error logs, stack traces, or CI failure output — triages and clusters errors, localizes root cause (file → function → line), enters plan mode with a structured fix + test proposal for human approval, writes a typed pytest regression test using mocks first, then implements a targeted code fix and verifies the test turns green.

name: security-mindset-master
description: Gates implementation of API endpoints, auth logic, database queries, and user input handling with threat surface analysis, secure defaults verification, and attacker's eye pass before any code ships.

name: compliance-check
description: Assesses US regulatory compliance likelihood for any consumer product before listing for US sale — classifies product category, determines applicable standards, gathers market evidence, and produces a structured verdict.

name: analyzing-dtc-stores
description: Produces an investor-grade teardown of a DTC brand from its public URL, covering brand, market, unit economics, supply chain, channel mix, marketing, reviews, agentic-commerce readiness, risks, and a falsifiable verdict.

name: audit-brand
description: Audits and evolves brand positioning, voice consistency, and messaging for an existing brand — runs a structured audit across positioning, voice, visual direction, and content pillars, then produces a scorecard with specific fix recommendations.

name: marketing-plan
description: Develops a full go-to-market marketing plan for a new online business covering ICP, competitive landscape, channel strategy, budget allocation, pricing, content strategy with retention, 90-day launch timeline, and KPI framework.

name: auto-research
description: Autonomously optimizes any Claude skill (SKILL.md) or CLAUDE.md file through a closed hypothesis→test→evaluate→keep/discard loop, producing an updated skill file and an iteration dashboard.

name: morning-brief
description: Generates a structured morning brief and writes it to the daily review file — pulls from current priorities, active task board, latest competitor digest, and content pipeline status.

name: stress-test
description: Three-phase strategic decision analysis combining verbalized sampling, customizable analytical lenses, and a structured decision brief — surfaces tail-distribution insights that mode-collapsed prompting misses.

name: analyzing-feedback
description: Parses raw customer feedback (app reviews, support tickets, interview transcripts) into categorized severity-ranked themes, scans the codebase to identify affected files, and proposes 3 structural edit options per theme.

name: find-my-business
description: Guides a founder interactively from 'no idea' through idea generation, rapid validation, and commitment to a business — synthesizing PG, YC, Lean Startup, Mom Test, JTBD, and gstack office-hours methodology.

name: html-output
description: Renders complex information as a single self-contained HTML file — dashboards, analytical reports, comparison tables, timelines, flow/sequence diagrams, and data explainers — using a brand-token design system, with optional Mermaid or Chart.js via CDN.

name: analysis-quality-review
description: Review-only multi-mode quality grader for reader-facing analytical deliverables — returns doc-type authoring templates, judges storylines before writing, and audits argument structure (Minto Pyramid, MECE, SCQA, Rumelt kernel) and readability (action titles, so-what, specificity, active voice) of finished documents.

name: write-report
description: Review-only multi-mode quality grader for reader-facing analytical deliverables — returns doc-type authoring templates, judges storylines before writing, and audits argument structure (Minto Pyramid, MECE, SCQA, Rumelt kernel) and readability (action titles, so-what, specificity, active voice, concept density, register/tone-of-voice) of finished documents. Canonical name going forward; analysis-quality-review is a retained alias.

name: analyze-industry
description: Orchestrates MBB sub-skills (BCG Strategy Palette, McKinsey G3 sizing, Porter Five Forces, Bain profit pools, arenas, S-curve, Helmer 7 Powers, JTBD) into a senior-analyst industry-structure brief shipped as a quality-reviewed HTML report ending in where-to-play / how-to-win.

name: reimagine-industry
description: Produces a ranked shortlist of venture concepts for disrupting an industry via a 6-phase workflow applying Blue Ocean ERRC, Aggregation Theory, Decoupling, Counter-positioning, Helmer 7 Powers, and Thiel's Secret.

name: assess-strategic-environment
description: Diagnoses the competitive environment using BCG's Strategy Palette — Classical / Adaptive / Visionary / Shaping / Renewal — via the predictability / malleability / harshness discipline, and emits a sub-skill routing matrix.

name: size-market
description: Granular market sizing using McKinsey G3 granular-growth decomposition plus an arenas qualification screen, with top-down and bottom-up triangulation — TAM/SAM/SOM and sub-segment growth rates with provenance tags on every numeric claim.

name: map-five-forces
description: Porter Five Forces analysis extended with complementors (sixth force) and AI-as-named-force; names the single governing force that determines an industry's profit distribution.

name: map-value-chain-profit-pools
description: Paired Porter value-chain decomposition and Bain/Gadiesh-Gilbert profit-pool overlay showing absolute EBIT / economic profit per stage and which stage holds structural profit concentration.

name: map-competitive-arena
description: Porter strategic-group map overlaid with McKinsey Arenas framing — identifies strategic groups, names a winner archetype, and assesses mobility barriers between adjacent groups.

name: analyze-trajectory
description: Forward-looking industry trajectory analysis — dual S-curve, Three Horizons overlay, discontinuities catalog, Helmer Power Progression, and base/bear/bull scenarios with named swing variables over a 5-year horizon.

name: assess-moat-sources
description: Assesses which of Helmer's 7 Powers protect incumbents in an industry, how durable each is over the analysis horizon, and which Power combination the winning archetype must hold.

name: analyze-demand
description: Structural demand-side analysis for an industry — Jobs-to-be-Done, JTBD-based segmentation, substitution risk per segment, willingness-to-pay drivers, and named leading demand signals.
